items.title: Tugboat? Pipeline? Why there's no quick fuel fix for the Nuyina

children.content: Australia's Antarctic icebreaker can't access the marine fuel depot near its home port in Hobart. It currently makes a 674 kilometre detour to Burnie, but are there other options?

image.altText: Front on view of an icebreaker ship.

items.title: TasTAFE plans to cut 118 full-time jobs by 2030, documents show

children.content: Documents show TasTAFE plans to cut 118 full-time equivalent positions, including 63 teaching roles by 2030, and course fees could go up by 7 per cent.

image.altText: A TasTAFE campus signage in Hobart.

items.title: EPA clashes with Greens on need for real-time tracking of salmon antibiotic use

children.content: The director of Tasmania's Environment Protection Authority rejects calls to release real-time information on the amount of antibiotics used by salmon producers, despite the companies already providing that information to other government bodies.

image.altText: A woman with glasses leans forwards and speaks into a microphone in a committee room.

items.title: 'Major shake-up': Councillors circle top spot as lord mayor bows out

children.content: After eight years in the top role, Anna Reynolds has announced she will not seek re-election in October. Her departure coincides with a notable reduction in the number of council representatives.

image.altText: Anna Reynolds

items.title: Missing Tasmanian devil likely 'having the time of her life', wildlife expert says

children.content: A Tasmanian devil missing on the Gold Coast is likely "absolutely fine" and "having the time of her life", according to wildlife expert Greg Irons.

articles.title: No sign of missing Tasmanian devil Mary but experts confident she will survive

items.title: Ancient tribes, military, trade and China's rise meet on this island

children.content: India's Great Nicobar Island is home to one of the world's last isolated tribes who are believed to have lived there for thousands of years, but a rising China is set to see it undergo a major transformation at the Indian government's insistence.

items.title: What's got the Pope and most Australians worried? This hyped new AI technology
